betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Satellite Sensors & Instruments§f questline covers the §6specialized modules§f that go into a satellite beyond the basic §6Satellite Primary Function§f — ore scanners, biome changers, mass detectors, atmospheric analyzers, and planetary mappers. These are added to a satellite at the §6Satellite Builder§f (10039) via its GUI.

§6Production Path (modular assembly)§f:
- Each sensor is a separate craftable item in AR:
  - §6Ore Scanner§f (advancedrocketry:orescanner) — recipes removed in `advancedRocketryTweaks.zs:136` (HBM's `sat_orescanner` is the canonical replacement)
  - §6Ore Mapper§f (advancedrocketry:satelliteprimaryfunction:4) — same
  - §6Biome Changer§f (advancedrocketry:biomechanger) — added as a separate quest at 10066
  - §6Composition Sensor§f (advancedrocketry:satelliteprimaryfunction:1) — a §6Precision Assembler§f recipe
  - §6Mass Scanner§f — uses §6Data Unit§f storage
- Sensors are inserted into a §6Satellite§f (10040) at the builder; the resulting satellite carries all configured sensors
- A satellite can hold up to §64 sensors§f plus 1 primary function

The sensor library enables the §6Ore Mapping Mission§f (chain), the §6Atmosphere Terraforming Mission§f (chain), and the §6Planetary Composition Analysis§f (chain). Each sensor adds a §6data tick§f to the satellite's collected data, which is then transmitted to the §6Satellite Terminal§f (10041).

This quest is a prerequisite for the §6Planet Holo Selector§f (chain) and the §6Interstellar Mission§f (chain). It is the most customizable quest in the AR progression because the player can mix and match sensors based on their mission goals.

§eTip§f: Combine an §6Ore Scanner§f with a §6Biome Changer§f in the same satellite to enable both ore discovery and biome modification from a single orbital asset. The §6Composition Sensor§f is required for §6asteroid mining§f because asteroids have no surface biome to map.
